assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, lines 8-9: "I search through the returned comments for any whose body starts with the marker string `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:`, as defined in `shared/description-digest-protocol.md`." This explicitly names both the marker string and the protocol file.

assertion_results.text: The response describes computing a digest of the current description (using scripts/sha256-digest.py or SHA-256) and comparing it with the stored digest
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, lines 32-38 describe computing the digest: "I extract the description field from the TC-9201 issue response, write it to a temporary file `/tmp/desc-TC-9201.txt`, and compute the digest: `python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/desc-TC-9201.txt`". Lines 44-48 compare the stored and computed hex digests side by side, confirming both are identical.

assertion_results.text: The response confirms the digests match and states it will proceed silently without prompting the user — no additional latency for the happy path
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, lines 49 and 52-53: "The digests match. The task description has not been modified since plan-feature created it." and "Digests match -- proceeding silently to subsequent steps without prompting the user. No additional latency is introduced for this happy-path verification."

assertion_results.text: The response does not alert the user or pause execution due to the digest check — it proceeds directly to subsequent steps
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, the outcome section (lines 52-53) states: "Digests match -- proceeding silently to subsequent steps without prompting the user." There is no alert, warning, or pause anywhere in the digest-match.md output. The plan.md file proceeds directly with implementation steps (Step 2 onward) without any mention of the digest check causing interruption.
